overall_assessment: Acceptable for the RR-AUTH-03 gate from an elegance and structural-soundness lens. The changed surfaces establish the intended hybrid model: token account binding, account-route mismatch rejection, and live role revalidation during account-scoped authorization. I found no release-blocking structural drift in the bounded package.
recommended_path: Proceed with the audit loop as non-blocking from the elegance lane. Track the remaining structural hardening opportunities as debt or deterministic guard candidates rather than reopening RR-AUTH-03 implementation.

findings.title: Token stamping service still permits unbound account-user tokens at the primitive API boundary
findings.rationale: The canonical package states that account-user bearer tokens are stamped with account_id at issue/stamp time, but TenantScopedAccessTokenService::stampAccountId returns silently when no explicit or current account can be resolved. Current account-scoped routes fail closed on missing binding through CheckUserAccess, so this is not a release blocker for the bounded account-route risk. Structurally, however, the low-level issuer remains more permissive than the canonical rule and relies on downstream route enforcement or caller discipline.
findings.suggested_action: For a follow-up hardening slice, split the token issuer API into explicit account-bound and tenant-only issue paths, or make account-user token issuance require an account_id unless an intentional non-account token mode is named and documented.

findings.title: Live account-role revalidation depends on an implicit Context flag set by middleware composition
findings.rationale: AccountUser::tokenCan only revalidates current-account permissions when Account::current exists and CheckUserAccess has set accountScopedAuthorization in Context. The current route fixtures route through the account middleware, and the evidence supports the bounded package. The structural risk is future drift: an account-scoped route using Sanctum abilities without the account middleware would bypass the live permission revalidation branch and trust token abilities after token->can succeeds.
findings.suggested_action: Add or extend a project architecture guardrail that rejects account-prefixed route groups or account-scoped ability middleware unless the account middleware is present before Sanctum ability checks.
